personalInfo.name: Karam Matar
personalInfo.title: Software Engineer
personalInfo.bio: Software engineer with a B.Sc. from BTH. Built a compiler from scratch in C++ and an AI-driven CI/CD automation system as a bachelor thesis. Internships at Softhouse (full-stack, security) and Trafikverket (DevOps tooling). Proficient in Python, C++ and TypeScript.
personalInfo.availableText: Open to full-time roles — preferably Sweden, open to relocate

paragraphs: I'm driven by building software that people actually use — taking an idea from nothing to a working product is the part of engineering I find most rewarding. Whether it's a compiler that executes code I defined the rules for, or a survey platform that replaced a company's existing workflow, I care most when the result matters to someone.

My background spans low-level systems and full-stack web development. I've written a compiler from scratch in C++, designed an AI-driven CI/CD pipeline as my bachelor thesis, identified and fixed security vulnerabilities in production code, and served as the communication link between developers and customers at Softhouse.
I'm currently sharpening my algorithms through competitive programming courses and looking for full-time engineering roles.

contactContent.sectionDescription: Feel free to reach out! Best way to reach me is through email.
contactContent.ctaDescription: Whether you have a question or just want to say hi, I'll try my best to get back to you as soon as possible!

skillsContent.learningTitle: Always learning and exploring new technologies. Currently working on:
currentlyLearning: Competitive Programming (Search, Dynamic Programming, Graphs & Flows)

footerContent.tagline: Software Engineer focused on systems programming, full-stack development, and AI-driven automation

description: Acted as communication lead throughout the project for a team of 11, and also served as rotating Scrum Master during one sprint — coordinating requirements, demos, and sprint updates between developers, the customer, and the Head of Department. The project was formally accepted as exceeding customer expectations.

Filed a security vulnerability report identifying missing server-side validation and cross-survey injection risks, then implemented backend fixes using Zod schemas to resolve critical issues before delivery.
Wrote Cypress E2E tests for core survey flows, built the configurable scale questions feature, and contributed to backend validation, PR reviews, and merge conflict resolution.

experiences.location: Remote via Blekinge Institute of Technology, Sweden
description: Collaborated with a team of six to build an Azure DevOps pipeline task that automatically scans third-party packages for known vulnerabilities across multiple repositories.
Refactored a legacy PowerShell script into a maintainable JavaScript codebase, enabling automated SBOM generation in JSON format within CI/CD workflows.
Proactively kept absent team members informed and followed up on blockers, helping maintain development velocity throughout the project.
Mentored first year students in C++ through lab supervision and one on one tutoring, focusing on core programming logic and best practices.
Assisted students in debugging and mastering low-level concepts, including pointers, memory management, and object-oriented design.
Guided students in analyzing program behavior and performance to ensure code correctness and efficiency.

details: Focus: systems programming, memory management, data structures, and building efficient software.
Bachelor Thesis: Design and evaluation of an AI-driven task-to-code feedback loop in CI/CD pipelines, developed in collaboration with Iquest AB.
Relevant Coursework: Compiler Design, Operating Systems, Algorithms & Data Structures, Linear Algebra.
Focus: data analysis and cleaning datasets, machine learning algorithms, secure software development, threat analysis, and systems design for games.
Relevant Coursework: Computer Vision & AI, Algorithm Design and Analysis, Security Programming, Game Design.

projects.title: ACID Bot — Bachelor Thesis
projects.description: Multi-agent AI system that fetches tasks from ClickUp via MCP, autonomously generates code fixes using Claude Code inside Docker, and opens pull requests via gh cli — with scheduled runs and a GitHub Actions pipeline option.
projects.longDescription: Designed and evaluated an autonomous AI feedback loop for CI/CD pipelines as a bachelor thesis, developed in collaboration with Iquest AB. The system uses an orchestrator script coordinating multiple agents: it connects to ClickUp via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to fetch task descriptions, generates code changes using Claude (initially via API calls, later migrated to Claude Code running directly in a Docker container), and manages repositories and pull requests through gh cli. The pipeline supports both scheduled automated runs via Docker and on-demand execution through a GitHub Actions workflow.

projects.title: Team Temp App — Softhouse
projects.description: Full-stack survey platform built for Softhouse with an admin portal for managing surveys, templates, branches, and users, and a respondent portal with gamification, leaderboards, and streak tracking.
projects.longDescription: A full-stack survey administration platform developed during a five-month internship at Softhouse. Features an admin portal for creating and managing surveys, templates, branches, and users, and a respondent portal with gamification elements including leaderboards and streak tracking. Supports dark and light themes, Swedish and English localization, and configurable question types including scale questions. Built with React and TypeScript on the frontend, Bun/Express and Prisma on the backend, and MongoDB as the database.

projects.title: Compiler from Scratch
projects.description: End-to-end compiler built from scratch in C++ — lexer, parser, semantic analysis, CFG-based IR, TAC generation, and a custom virtual machine — all without external parser generators.
projects.longDescription: Built an end-to-end compiler in C++ from scratch, implementing lexical analysis, recursive-descent parsing, semantic analysis, control-flow graph based intermediate representation, three-address code generation, and a custom virtual machine for execution — all without external parser generators or compiler toolkits.

projects.title: VulnerabilityChecker 2.0
projects.description: Azure DevOps pipeline task that scans third-party packages for known vulnerabilities and generates SBOM files — refactored from a legacy PowerShell script into a maintainable JavaScript codebase for Trafikverket.
projects.longDescription: Designed and implemented an Azure DevOps pipeline task for Trafikverket that detects vulnerabilities in third-party dependencies across multiple repositories. Refactored from a legacy PowerShell script into JavaScript, the tool parses dependency manifests, queries vulnerability data, and generates Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) files in JSON format for CI/CD workflows.

projects.title: GyaGame
projects.description: 2D semi-open-world shooter game in Java using a provided graphics library, featuring game logic, entity management, collision systems, and player interaction.
projects.longDescription: A Java game project featuring core gameplay systems such as movement, shooting, and game logic, built using a provided graphics library.

projects.title: ShowcaseSERL
projects.description: Full-stack web application for BTH's Software Engineering Research Lab to showcase student and researcher projects, featuring dynamic filtering, detailed project views, and an auto-scrolling kiosk mode for lobby displays.
projects.longDescription: A Next.js web application built for BTH's Software Engineering Research Lab (SERL). Features dynamic project listings with category filtering, individual project detail pages, and a kiosk mode with auto-scrolling designed for lobby display screens. Built with server-side rendering for fast initial loads and responsive design for both desktop kiosks and mobile browsing.

projects.title: Space Invaders
projects.description: Created a Space Invaders game in C++ using the SFML graphics library, implementing core game loops, collision detection, and rendering.
projects.longDescription: A Space Invaders clone built in C++ using SFML, focusing on core game loop, input handling, collision logic, and classic arcade gameplay.

projects.title: Death Marching Souls
projects.description: Developed a 3D action-platformer featuring player progression, enemy AI, boss encounters, and UI systems, implementing health management, experience-based leveling, and modular enemy behavior.
projects.longDescription: A 3D Unity game featuring traversal, progression, and boss combat, built to practice gameplay systems including health management, experience-based leveling, and modular enemy behavior.

achievements.title: Hollytech
achievements.result: First place winner — built a winning prototype in a competitive, time-constrained team event

certificates.title: Teaching Assistant Certificate
certificates.description: Recognition of valuable commitment as a supervisor in the Department of Software Engineering, supporting C++ students with great responsibility and pedagogical ability.

certificates.title: Elements of AI
certificates.description: Completed the Elements of AI course with commendable results, equivalent to 2 ECTS credits.

seo.title: Karam Matar | Software Engineer
seo.description: Karam Matar is a software engineer with a B.Sc. in Software Engineering from BTH, focused on full-stack development, AI-driven automation, DevOps tooling, and game development.
